content:
# Apply Fixes

## Change Summary

Updated `src/adapters/process_backend.rs` so failed backend invocations no longer flow through success cleanup. Failure paths now call a dedicated `cleanup_failed_invocation()` helper that moves schema/last-message temp files into `runtime/failed/`, writes combined stdout/stderr to `<invocation_id>.failed.raw`, and preserves originals if a move or copy fails. Successful invocations still use normal cleanup. Added unit tests covering failure artifact preservation, failed-move fallback, and success-path deletion.

## Steps

0. [Completed] Inspect `src/adapters/process_backend.rs` failure and cleanup paths to confirm where failed invocations still deleted temp artifacts.
1. [Completed] Refactor failure handling so non-zero exits and post-run parsing/read failures preserve artifacts in `runtime/failed/` and emit a `.failed.raw` capture instead of deleting temp files.
2. [Completed] Add focused tests for failed-artifact preservation, move-failure fallback, and normal success cleanup behavior.

## Validation Evidence

- `nix-shell -p cargo rustc --run 'cargo test cleanup_failed_invocation -- --nocapture'` passed: 2 tests passed, 0 failed.
- `nix-shell -p cargo rustc --run 'cargo test cleanup_deletes_codex_temp_files_on_success -- --nocapture'` passed: 1 test passed, 0 failed.
- `nix-shell -p cargo rustc --run 'cargo test adapters::process_backend::tests -- --nocapture'` passed: 26 tests passed, 0 failed.
- Review outcome was `approved`; reviewer confirmed failure paths in `invoke()` and `finish()` now use `cleanup_failed_invocation()` without a follow-up success cleanup.

## Outstanding Risks

- Validation did not include a full end-to-end backend invocation; coverage is limited to targeted `process_backend` unit tests run under `nix-shell` because `cargo` is not available directly on PATH in this environment.
